title: Incident Lifecycle States
description: Machine-readable state model for the synthetic Azure Identity Threat Detection & SOAR Lab. All examples are fictional (contoso.com personas, TEST-NET documentation IP ranges); nothing has run against a production tenant. SLA values are the same four-band model enforced in code (src/incident_builder.py, SLA_MATRIX) and reported daily (output/daily_security_report.md). The worked reference incident is CP-INC-2001.
sla_note: Per-state SLAs below are operational targets for time spent in that state. The overall acknowledge/resolve SLAs by severity (P1 ack 15m/resolve 4h; P2 30m/8h; P3 4h/72h; P4 24h/7d) are defined in SEVERITY_MODEL.md and the SLA_MATRIX; the two are consistent and must not drift.

roles.SOAR: Automated orchestration; performs only read-only, reversible, no-approval actions (page, ticket, enrich, and session revocation at Critical).
roles.DRI: Directly Responsible Individual; the single accountable owner of the incident end-to-end.
roles.DRI + specialist on-call: DRI approves and owns; networking/identity/platform on-call executes their scoped action.

entry_criteria: A detection (DET-00x / CP-DET-00x) fires, or the correlation engine links multiple alerts into one incident (e.g. CP-DET-008).
Incident record does not yet exist or has just been created by the engine.
exit_criteria: An accountable DRI has acknowledged the page.
The incident has an assigned ID and entities have been enriched (read-only).
Only reversible, no-approval automation has run (page, ticket, enrich; session revocation at Critical).

sla.target: Page delivered and DRI acknowledgement within the severity acknowledge SLA (P1 15m, P2 30m, P3 4h, P4 24h).
sla.escalation: No acknowledgement within the ack SLA auto-escalates to secondary DRI, then team lead (P1).

states.cp_inc_2001_example: 10:40 correlated Critical incident raised (CP-DET-008); 10:41 DRI paged and acknowledged.

entry_criteria: DRI has acknowledged and opened the incident and its enrichment comment.
AI triage briefing (output/ai/) is available to read as a first take.
exit_criteria: Severity assigned per SEVERITY_MODEL.md (P1/P2/P3/P4).
Incident confirmed real, OR closed as a false positive with a recorded reason that feeds tuning.
AI briefing has been verified against raw evidence (model input is attacker-influenceable).
A least-destructive-first containment plan exists and scope-based escalations have been triggered.

sla.target: Complete triage within the first 15 minutes for P1 (first-15-minutes checklist); proportionally longer for lower severities.
sla.escalation: If triage cannot be completed solo within the window, pull in the secondary DRI.

states.cp_inc_2001_example: Privileged identity + high-privilege service principal + internet-exposed management port => unambiguous P1; network on-call and app owner engaged.

entry_criteria: Incident confirmed real with a severity and a containment plan.
Evidence-collection checklist is being worked (capture before rotate).
exit_criteria: Attacker can no longer act: no new tokens issued, no live internet-exposed surface, no usable attacker-added credential.
Every state-mutating action was approval-gated and snapshotted its prior state before executing.
Evidence captured before any credential rotation or rule revert.

sla.target: Begin containment within the first 30 minutes for P1 (first-30-minutes checklist); complete well inside the resolve SLA.
sla.escalation: Resolve SLA at 75% elapsed notifies the team lead.

states.cp_inc_2001_example: 10:43 sessions revoked (auto); 10:52 NSG reverted; 11:04 SP credential removed and secrets rotated; 11:10 Owner grant removed and PIM role deactivated; 11:20 host JIT-locked.

entry_criteria: Blast radius is capped (CONTAIN exit criteria met).
Evidence for all touched entities has been preserved.
exit_criteria: All attacker-introduced credentials, objects and configuration removed (added client secret, Owner grant, temporary NSG rule).
Every credential the attacker touched or could have touched is rotated.
Persistence hunt complete: re-enabling everything would not let the attacker back in.
If a new foothold is found, drop back to CONTAIN rather than advancing.

sla.target: Within the resolve SLA for the severity; no fixed sub-target because thoroughness governs, not the clock.
sla.escalation: Any newly discovered persistence re-opens CONTAIN and re-notifies the team lead.

states.cp_inc_2001_example: Attacker-added client secret on sp-infra-deploy removed and remaining secrets rotated from key vault; Owner assignment unwound; PIM role deactivated.

entry_criteria: A known-good baseline for affected services is identified.
exit_criteria: Affected services restored to a known-good state and business owner confirms normal operation.

Verification passes: detections re-run over the post-containment window return silent; no new tokens post-revocation; no successful inbound login to the exposed endpoint; no residual unexpected SP credentials.
Affected identity restored with phishing-resistant MFA and a password reset.

sla.target: Complete recovery and verification within the resolve SLA for the severity.
sla.escalation: Failed verification returns the incident to ERADICATE (or CONTAIN if a live foothold reappears).

states.cp_inc_2001_example: Confirmed no successful RDP login reached vm-dc-mgmt-01; NSG free of any internet-wide management rule; chris.walker@contoso.com restored with phishing-resistant MFA.

entry_criteria: Services recovered and all verification checks passed.
exit_criteria: Blameless RCA task raised (full RCA due within five business days).
Resolution note issued to the incident channel and stakeholders.
Action items recorded with a single owner and a due date each.
SLA outcome (acknowledge and resolve) recorded for the weekly operations review; any breach carries a reason.

sla.target: Close once verification is complete and the RCA task exists; full RCA written within five business days.
sla.escalation: SLA breaches and false-positive closes are surfaced at the weekly operations review.

states.cp_inc_2001_example: Incident closed after verification; blameless RCA raised with five owned, dated action items (see RCA_PROCESS.md).

transitions.on: DRI acknowledges and opens the incident
Severity set and incident confirmed real
Confirmed false positive (reason recorded, feeds tuning)
Attacker can no longer act; blast radius capped
